title: Border Gateway Protocol
description: The routing protocol of the internet — connects autonomous systems worldwide.
fullExplanation: BGP is the only EGP (Exterior Gateway Protocol) in use today. It connects ~100,000 Autonomous Systems (ASes) — each AS is a network under one administrative control (ISP, company, university). BGP peers exchange reachability information: 'I can reach these prefixes via this AS path'. BGP selects the best path using a complex decision process. eBGP runs between different ASes, iBGP runs within the same AS.

messageTypes.description: Establish BGP session — exchange AS number, BGP version, hold time, Router ID
Advertise new prefixes or withdraw previously advertised prefixes
Sent every 1/3 of hold time to maintain session (default every 60s)

keyFacts: RPKI (Resource Public Key Infrastructure) prevents BGP hijacking
Pakistan Telecom hijacked YouTube via BGP in 2008
BGP communities: 32-bit tags for policy control
Route reflectors eliminate iBGP full mesh requirement
